title: Encode pit-service carryover anomalies
summary: F1 stops are not identical tyre swaps: front-wing adjustments and operational issues can change the next stint context.
rationale: F1 stops are not identical tyre swaps: front-wing adjustments and operational issues can change the next stint context. The dataset has no stop duration, but a pit-lap LapTime residual, Position_Change, and post-stop pace can proxy abnormal service, damage repair, or compromised rejoin.
implementation_hint: For each Race_Year-Driver sequence, create lagged features from the most recent PitStop row: pit-lap time residual versus race/lap median, position loss at stop, compound changed flag, laps since abnormal stop, and whether post-stop first two laps were slow versus field median.
expected_effect: May separate planned strategy stops from incident/repair stops, improving ranking after unusual pit events and in races with multiple stops.
risk: Needs careful causal shifting: only use current and previous rows for training features, never next-lap target information.
